news.headline: Alphabet raises 2026 AI capex guidance to as much as $205 billion after Google Cloud growth surges 82 percent
news.summary: Alphabet raised its full-year 2026 capital expenditure guidance to a range of $195 billion to $205 billion, up from $180 billion to $190 billion set just one quarter earlier, during its Q2 2026 earnings call on July 22, 2026. The increase came even as Google Cloud revenue grew 82 percent year over year to $24.8 billion and total company revenue reached $119.8 billion, beating analyst estimates of about $117 billion. Alphabet CFO Anat Ashkenazi told analysts that demand for AI infrastructure still outpaces the company's investment and warned 2027 spending will increase significantly further. Alphabet shares fell about 5 percent in after-hours trading despite the earnings beat, as investors focused on free cash flow compression from the capex hike. The company funded part of the buildout with $30.5 billion in stock issuance and $19.1 billion in convertible preferred stock during the quarter.
news.relevance_justification: This is the clearest read yet on how far the AI capex arms race has to run, and it is not slowing down. If you sell into hyperscaler supply chains, budget planning for 2027 needs to assume this trajectory keeps climbing, not plateauing. Enterprise buyers should also note the market punished the beat itself, which tells you Wall Street is now more worried about ROI timing than about growth.

news.headline: White House accuses Moonshot AI of distilling Anthropic's Fable to build Kimi K3, Treasury threatens sanctions
news.summary: Michael Kratsios, director of the White House Office of Science and Technology Policy, said on July 22, 2026 that the US government has information Chinese lab Moonshot AI covertly distilled Anthropic's Fable model to build its open-weight Kimi K3 model. Kratsios also alleged Moonshot acquired Nvidia GB300 chips banned for export to China and accessed them in Thailand. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the same day that sanctions against Chinese AI companies remain on the table. Moonshot released Kimi K3, a 2.8 trillion parameter open-weight model, on July 16, 2026, and it has drawn attention for benchmark performance close to Anthropic's and OpenAI's frontier models. Moonshot and the Chinese Embassy in Washington did not respond to requests for comment.
news.relevance_justification: This escalates US-China AI tension from corporate accusation to a stated sanctions threat, which is a real policy lever, not just posturing. If you rely on open-weight Chinese models in any product pipeline, this is the moment to reassess vendor risk and export-control exposure. Expect this story to keep moving fast given Moonshot's July 27 full weights release is still pending.

news.headline: OpenAI unveils $30 billion Project Camellia data center campus in Georgia
news.summary: OpenAI announced on July 22, 2026 plans to build Project Camellia, a 3.2-gigawatt data center campus on roughly 1,400 acres in Effingham County, Georgia, near Savannah. The company has committed at least $20 billion to the project to qualify for a local tax incentive, with Bloomberg reporting total costs could exceed $30 billion at full build-out. OpenAI signed a 25-year power agreement with Georgia Power and pledged $80 million in community benefits plus up to $71 million in Codex credits for Georgia students. Delivery of the 3.2 gigawatts is expected in phases between 2028 and 2032, and OpenAI is still seeking financing and construction partners. This is OpenAI's first data center campus that it is designing and building itself.
news.relevance_justification: OpenAI is now self-building infrastructure rather than only leasing it, which signals how central compute ownership has become to its competitive strategy. Anyone tracking power and land constraints in AI buildouts should watch how quickly Effingham County's grid and community relations hold up as a template for future megaprojects. The multi-year timeline is also a reminder that today's compute scarcity has no quick fix.

news.headline: Amazon confirms new layoffs inside its AGI organization
news.summary: Amazon confirmed on July 23, 2026 that it is cutting jobs within its Artificial General Intelligence organization, without disclosing the number of employees or specific roles affected. Reuters reported the cuts hit teams working on model customization and post-training, including groups led by AGI Data Services VP Adeeb Shanaa and AGI Information VP Vishal Sharma. The reductions add to more than 30,000 job cuts at Amazon over the prior ten months, including 14,000 in October 2025 and 16,000 in January 2026. An Amazon spokesperson said the company is sharpening focus on initiatives that matter most to customers while continuing to invest in AI. The cuts come days ahead of Amazon's Q2 earnings release on July 30 and amid a planned $200 billion in 2026 capital expenditure.
news.relevance_justification: Even the companies pouring the most money into AI are trimming the teams building it, which tells you this is about prioritization, not retreat. If your organization is restructuring around AI, Amazon's pattern of repeated smaller cuts alongside massive capex is a template worth studying. Watch which roles get cut versus which get funded, that split is the real signal about where AI work is heading.

news.headline: OpenAI launches Presence, an enterprise agent platform proven with BBVA, SoftBank and IAG
news.summary: OpenAI introduced Presence on July 22, 2026, an enterprise product for deploying AI agents that handle customer and internal workflows with policies, guardrails and escalation rules. Presence already powers OpenAI's own English-language phone support line, resolving 75 percent of inbound issues without human help and cutting handoffs by 15 percentage points in 10 days after launch. Design partners include BBVA in Mexico for banking support, SoftBank for Japanese-language customer service, and IAG's Retail Insurance Australia for claims support during severe weather events. Presence is available today for voice and chat agents through a limited general availability program led by OpenAI Forward Deployed Engineers and select systems integrators, and is not yet self-serve. The launch places OpenAI alongside Google Cloud's Gemini Enterprise, Meta's Business Agent Platform and Nvidia-ServiceNow's Project Arc in the enterprise agent category.
news.relevance_justification: Four major players just launched competing enterprise agent platforms in the same month, so this category is no longer experimental, it is becoming table stakes for large enterprises. If you run customer support, banking, or insurance operations, the named deployments here give you a concrete benchmark for what production-grade agent performance actually looks like today. The bigger story is that OpenAI is positioning itself as a services company, not just a model vendor.

news.headline: OpenAI launches Health in ChatGPT for US users
news.summary: OpenAI rolled out Health in ChatGPT to US users on July 23, 2026, letting people securely connect Apple Health data and supported medical records from US hospital systems, One Medical, or Function Health. More than 300 million people ask ChatGPT health-related questions weekly, and OpenAI said early testing found over 70 percent of health conversations happened outside its earlier dedicated health space, prompting the integration into normal chats. GPT-5.6 Sol, available to paid users, is described as OpenAI's strongest health model yet, outperforming GPT-5.5 on the HealthBench Professional evaluation developed with physicians. OpenAI said connected medical records and Apple Health data are not used to train its foundation models or target ads, and users can disconnect data at any time. The feature is live on web and iOS for Free, Go, Plus, and Pro plans for logged-in users 18 and older in the US.
news.relevance_justification: This pushes ChatGPT directly into personal health records at massive scale, which raises the stakes on both usefulness and liability if the model gets something wrong. Healthcare and consumer product teams should watch adoption numbers closely since this could reshape how patients arrive at appointments already informed, or misinformed. Privacy design choices here will likely become the reference point regulators and competitors measure against.

news.headline: Nvidia signs $5.5 billion combined deal with data center operator IREN
news.summary: Nvidia and IREN announced a strategic partnership to deploy up to 5 gigawatts of Nvidia's DSX-branded AI infrastructure across IREN's global data center pipeline, with future deployments focused on IREN's 2-gigawatt Sweetwater campus in Texas. As part of the deal, IREN issued Nvidia a five-year right to purchase up to 30 million shares at $70 each, giving Nvidia the option to invest up to $2.1 billion in the company. Separately, IREN signed a five-year, $3.4 billion contract to provide Nvidia managed GPU cloud services using Blackwell systems at its Childress, Texas campus for Nvidia's internal AI and research workloads. IREN shares climbed sharply following the announcement, extending its transition from a former bitcoin mining company into an AI infrastructure operator. IREN previously signed a $9.7 billion multi-year cloud deal with Microsoft.
news.relevance_justification: Nvidia keeps recycling capital into its own customer base through equity stakes plus supply contracts, and that circular financing pattern is worth watching closely if you are assessing how sustainable the current AI infrastructure boom really is. For anyone evaluating neocloud vendors, IREN's rapid pivot from bitcoin mining to a multi-billion-dollar Nvidia and Microsoft partner is a fast-moving case study in how compute scarcity is reshaping who gets funded.

news.headline: Monday.com cuts 20 percent of workforce to restructure around AI
news.summary: Israeli workplace software company Monday.com announced it is cutting 620 jobs globally, about 20 percent of its workforce, to realign its operational structure around AI capabilities, according to reporting published July 23, 2026. Co-founders Roy Mann and Eran Zinman told employees in a letter that it was the most painful decision in the company's history. Leadership said the majority of the financial savings will be reinvested into product development and internal AI technology rather than treated purely as cost-cutting. In internal communications, leadership pushed back on the idea that internal AI adoption directly caused the cuts, instead framing the move as reducing corporate friction as AI products require deeper implementation support. The company said some existing roles will change while new positions tied to AI-enabled products are created.
news.relevance_justification: A B2B SaaS vendor cutting a fifth of its staff while explicitly citing AI-era restructuring is a sharper data point than the usual vague AI efficiency language other companies use. If you run a SaaS product organization, this is a signal that go-to-market and support functions are being redesigned around AI-native delivery models faster than headcount can adjust. Watch whether this becomes a template other mid-size SaaS companies follow this year.

news.headline: DeepSeek retires legacy chat and reasoner model names on July 24 deadline
news.summary: DeepSeek's legacy API aliases deepseek-chat and deepseek-reasoner are fully retired and inaccessible after July 24, 2026 at 15:59 UTC, according to the company's official API documentation. The aliases currently route to DeepSeek-V4-Flash in non-thinking and thinking modes respectively, and developers must migrate to the explicit model IDs deepseek-v4-pro or deepseek-v4-flash. DeepSeek shipped the V4 Preview on April 24, 2026 with V4-Pro at 1.6 trillion total parameters and V4-Flash at 284 billion parameters, both open-weight under MIT license with a 1 million token default context window. No official date for a stable, non-preview V4 release has been confirmed, despite some reports pointing to mid-July. The migration requires only a one-line change to the model parameter, with the same base URL and API key.
news.relevance_justification: This is a mandatory, hard-deadline change for anyone running production workloads on DeepSeek's API, and there is no extension. Engineering teams using deepseek-chat or deepseek-reasoner in scripts need to update model IDs today, not next sprint, or calls will start failing. It is also a reminder that even fast-moving open-weight vendors expect enterprises to keep pace with their versioning cadence.

news.headline: Intel launches fresh layoffs in Data Center and AI Group ahead of Q2 earnings
news.summary: Intel began another round of layoffs affecting employees in its Data Center and AI Group, reported July 23, 2026, just before the company's second-quarter earnings release after market close the same day. The cuts are part of CEO Lip-Bu Tan's broader restructuring effort, which has already included plans to reduce Intel's global workforce by about 15 percent since he succeeded Pat Gelsinger as CEO in March 2025. The Data Center and AI Group generated $5.05 billion in revenue in the first quarter of 2026, up 22 percent year over year. Wall Street expected adjusted earnings of 22 cents per share on revenue of $14.45 billion for the quarter, compared with a loss of 10 cents per share a year earlier. The cuts add to a broader industry trend, with Layoffs.fyi tracking 121,326 tech sector job cuts so far in 2026, nearly matching the 122,606 recorded across all of 2025.
news.relevance_justification: Cutting inside a growing, profitable division right before earnings tells you this is about margin discipline under Tan's turnaround plan, not weak demand for AI chips. Anyone in semiconductor supply chains or enterprise procurement should track how Intel's AI unit headcount trends against its revenue growth, since it signals how the company plans to compete against Nvidia and AMD on cost structure. It is also another entry in a year where AI is cited as the leading reason for tech layoffs.

news.headline: Fireworks AI raises $1.5 billion Series D at $17.5 billion valuation
news.summary: Fireworks AI, an enterprise inference and model-serving platform founded by former PyTorch engineers, announced a $1.505 billion Series D funding round on July 16, 2026 at a $17.5 billion valuation. The round was led by Atreides Management, Index Ventures, and TCV, with participation from Nvidia, Lightspeed Venture Partners, Bessemer Venture Partners, Insight Partners, and others. The raise coincides with Fireworks surpassing $1 billion in annualized revenue run rate, up 5 times year over year, and the company says it now processes more than 40 trillion tokens per day for customers including Cursor, Harvey, Uber, Shopify, Doximity and Revolut. Fireworks has now raised more than $1.8 billion in total funding, up from a roughly $4 billion valuation just seven months earlier at its Series C. CEO Lin Qiao said the company is betting on enterprises building their own specialized intelligence rather than renting general-purpose models from large labs.
news.relevance_justification: A 4.4x valuation jump in seven months on the inference and specialized-model-serving thesis says the market is starting to bet against the just-rent-a-frontier-model strategy for enterprise AI. If you are deciding whether to build custom models on your own data versus buying access to GPT or Claude, this round is a strong signal that infrastructure investors think the specialized route has real staying power. It is also one of the largest AI infrastructure rounds of the year outside the big labs themselves.

news.headline: AMD signs $5 billion Anthropic partnership and lands Microsoft deal for Helios chips in same week
news.summary: AMD confirmed a strategic partnership under which it will invest up to $5 billion in Anthropic while Anthropic deploys up to two gigawatts of AMD's Helios AI infrastructure, using MI400-series Instinct GPUs, reported around July 22, 2026. Unlike AMD's prior arrangements with OpenAI and Meta, which included warrants for up to 160 million shares each, the Anthropic investment carries no warrants, only a capped cash investment. The deal lands before Helios has even reached general commercial availability, expected in the second half of 2026, giving AMD two marquee enterprise validators, Microsoft and Anthropic, within days of each other. AMD CEO Lisa Su posted that she was thrilled Helios would help power Claude at gigawatt scale. The partnership positions AMD as Nvidia's most serious near-term challenger in large-scale AI compute.
news.relevance_justification: Anthropic diversifying its chip supply away from Nvidia into AMD signals real concern about single-vendor dependency at the frontier lab level, and that has ripple effects for anyone planning multi-year compute contracts. If you are negotiating GPU supply deals, this shows top labs are now willing to bet meaningfully on a pre-GA chip platform to avoid being squeezed by one supplier. Watch whether AMD can convert this momentum into broader market share before Helios even ships at scale.
